snippet.title: The Trail of Tears - One Minute History
snippet.description:
Between 1830 and 1850, approximately 100,000 Native Americans were forced to abandon their ancestral homelands in the southeastern United States and relocate to Indian Territory in present-day Oklahoma. 

 The relocation, known as the Trail of Tears, was a result of the Indian Removal Act of 1830, signed into law by President Andrew Jackson. The Cherokee Nation challenged the act in court, but their efforts were unsuccessful.  

As a result, the Cherokee and other tribes were forcibly removed from their homes and marched westward on foot, with thousands dying from disease, hunger, and exposure along the way. 

The Trail of Tears was a devastating and tragic event in American history, forever altering the lives of Native American tribes and their descendants. Today, it serves as a reminder of the destructive effects of colonialism and forced assimilation on indigenous peoples.

Read by Adam Giles - TWITTER @adzreflects - adzreflex@googlemail.com

snippet.title: What NOT To Say to a Doubting Christian
snippet.description:
Recently on the Cross Examined podcast with Frank Turek, Dr. Gary Habermas said that even if the Bible was unreliable, one should still be a Christian because of the evidence for the resurrection that even skeptical scholars grant. Here's why I think he's wrong about that, and why I am not a fan of the minimal facts approach to the resurrection in general. I also answer objections to taking a more maximal approach. 

Credit to Dr. Jonathan McLatchie and Dr. Lydia McGrew on many of the points I raise here.

00:34 Habermas Clip
02:43 What is Minimal Facts?
04:35 Minimal Facts' Weakness
07:45 We Need a Reliable Bible
09:47 Max Data takes too long?
12:06 Rabbit Trails? Contradictions

snippet.title: Ghost photo: The story of the Amityville Horror House Ghost
snippet.description:
Support the channel by subscribing! 🙏

On November 13, 1974, six members of the DeFeo family—the father and the mother, two brothers, and two sisters—were massacred in Amityville, Long Island.
After arriving on the scene, authorities discovered all of the victims lying face down in their beds.
They were each shot dead with a Marlin 336C rifle around 3 a.m., and the investigation revealed that Louise DeFeo and her daughter, Allison, were awake at the time of their deaths.
It was very suspicious from the beginning; initial interviews at the police station had already exposed inconsistencies in Ronald DeFeo Jr.’s story, and the man confessed to the murders the next day.
Two years later, Gene Campbell, a photographer George Lutz had hired, allegedly went to the house and captured something truly scary.
According to the story, Campbell had set up an automatic infrared camera on the second floor for the night, and one of the photos showed what appeared to be a young boy with glowing eyes looking out of a dark room. #ghost #mystery #unsolved #amityville #unsolved

Dr. Jeff Meldrum is a Full Professor of Anatomy & Anthropology at Idaho State University (since 1993). He teaches human anatomy in the graduate health professions programs. His research encompasses questions of vertebrate evolutionary morphology generally, primate locomotor adaptations more particularly, and especially the emergence of modern human bipedalism. His co-edited volume, From Biped to Strider: the Emergence of Modern Human Walking, Running, and Resource Transport, proposes a more recent innovation of modern striding gait than previously assumed.

snippet.title: $NEE NextEra Energy Q3 2023 Earnings Conference Call
snippet.description:
10/24/2023 Q&A: 30:10
NextEra Energy, Inc., through its subsidiaries, generates, transmits, distributes, and sells electric power to retail and wholesale customers in North America. The company generates electricity through wind, solar, nuclear, coal, and natural gas facilities. It also develops, constructs, and operates long-term contracted assets that consists of clean energy solutions, such as renewable generation facilities, battery storage projects, and electric transmission facilities; sells energy commodities; and owns, develops, constructs, manages and operates electric generation facilities in wholesale energy markets. As of December 31, 2022, the company had approximately 32,100 megawatts of net generating capacity; approximately 88,000 circuit miles of transmission and distribution lines; and 871 substations. It serves approximately 12 million people through approximately 5.8 million customer accounts in the east and lower west coasts of Florida. The company was formerly known as FPL Group, Inc. and changed its name to NextEra Energy, Inc. in 2010. NextEra Energy, Inc. was founded in 1925 and is headquartered in Juno Beach, Florida.
#nextera #earnings #fundamentals #earningscall #nee

snippet.title: Epic Old West Showdown: Arizona Ranger vs Skunk
snippet.description:
Ed Scarborough cut his teeth chasing outlaws like Black Jack Ketchum & the Wild Bunch when he was still a teenager. His own father was the legendary lawman who killed the man who killed John Wesley Hardin and Ed would go on to become a deputy sheriff, constable, Arizona Ranger, and according to those who knew him, he was born to a saddle, an excellent marksman, and did not know the meaning of the word fear. Make no mistake about it, Ed Scarborough took down many a sassy bandit over the course of his career. Be that as it may, the toughest opponents he’d ever face turned out to be himself and a rabid skunk. Toss in a little bit of cross-dressing, a shootout with a bicycle, some good ole fashioned homicide and a daring prison escape, and we’ve got what may be the most absurd tale I’ve ever had the pleasure to share. Trust me; you’re not going to want to skip this one. #history #wildwest #oldwesthistory

snippet.description:

book  on left to tell by immaculee ilibagiza.
cabin chat kicks off a new series with its adventures in reading and immaculee ilibagiza's "left to tell" at .

 
📚 Dive into the gripping story of survival, forgiveness, and hope in "Left to Tell" by Immaculée Ilibagiza. This powerful memoir takes you on a journey through unimaginable horrors and extraordinary resilience.

🌟 SUMMARY:
Immaculée's life in Rwanda took a horrifying turn during the 1994 genocide. As a young Tutsi woman, she found herself trapped in a tiny bathroom with seven other women, hiding from the ruthless Interahamwe militia. For 91 days, Immaculée clung to faith, prayer, and an unwavering will to survive amidst the brutality surrounding her.

🙏 WITNESS THE UNBREAKABLE SPIRIT:
Immaculée's harrowing account reveals the atrocities of the genocide, but also showcases the strength of the human spirit when faced with the darkest of circumstances. Her unwavering faith in God and the power of forgiveness helped her find hope in the midst of despair.

💔 HEARTBREAK AND HEALING:
From witnessing the loss of her family to finding solace in forgiveness, Immaculée's journey will leave you in awe of her resilience. Her story is a reminder that even in the face of unimaginable tragedy, love and compassion can heal wounds and rebuild lives.

🌈 A MESSAGE OF HOPE:
"Left to Tell" is more than just a tale of survival; it's a beacon of hope for humanity. Immaculée's courage and determination to rise above hatred and violence serve as an inspiration to embrace love and unity in a fractured world.

📖 Whether you're seeking a gripping memoir, a testament to the human spirit, or a message of forgiveness and hope, "Left to Tell" is a must-read. Immerse yourself in this incredible true story of one woman's survival and transformation amidst the darkest of times.

snippet.title: S4EP05 - The Science of Sasquatch - Dr. Jeff Meldrum
snippet.description:
On this episode of the Paranormal Portal Podcast, we welcome Dr. Jeff Meldrum to the show. Dr. Meldrum is a man that needs no introduction in the world of Bigfoot/Sasquatch. He is a regular voice of authority on pretty much every and any show that seeks to explore the validity of this incredible legend. Dr. Meldrum has faced a mountain of adversity for his stance regarding the potential validity of the Sasquatch Phenomena, but he has persevered both professionally and personally in the face of this incredible opposition. He has brought so much credibility to a topic that has been completely dismissed by the scientific community, as a whole. Through countless appearances on Television and documentaries and authoring and incredible book "Sasquatch: Legend Meets Science". My discussion with Dr. Meldrum was an incredible experience, not only to hear his incredible insights on the Sasquatch phenomena, but to get a chance to know the man, himself. Join us for this amazing journey into the Science of Sasquatch and so much more. Enter the Paranormal Portal...if you dare!

snippet.title: Horror author Stephen Graham Jones on what makes a story scary and how he writes so prolifically
snippet.description:
(This episode was originally released as a podcast on 7 August 2020)

Stephen Graham Jones is the author of twenty-five or so novels and collections, and there’s some novellas and comic books in there as well. Stephen’s been an NEA recipient, has won the Texas Institute of Letters Award for Fiction, the Independent Publishers Award for Multicultural Fiction, a Bram Stoker Award, four This is Horror Awards, and he’s been a finalist for the Shirley Jackson Award and the World Fantasy Award. He’s also made Bloody Disgusting’s Top Ten Horror Novels, and his latest book, The Only Good Indians is already getting rave reviews.

We had an inspiring chat with Stephen about how he manages to write so many great stories, and get his insights on what makes a scary horror story. We also hear his amazing story about how his first novel came about, discuss how he writes so quickly, and of course learn whether he prefers a real book or an ebook...

Links:

Buy The Only Good Indians and Stephen's other books: https://www.amazon.co.uk/Stephen-Graham-Jones/e/B001JP9YPW
Visit Stephen's website: https://www.demontheory.net/

snippet.title: I read one of my favorite books this year so far! September Book Wrap-Up Horror and science fiction
snippet.description:
Welcome to The Vintage Viking! Dive into the thrilling worlds of science fiction and horror literature with me as I explore a galaxy of books, sharing my reviews, recommendations, and captivating book hauls. Immerse yourself in the realms of imagination as I delve into the darkest corners of horror and the boundless possibilities of science fiction. Join me on this literary journey, where every video is a portal to new adventures, gripping stories, and thought-provoking tales. Whether you're a seasoned bookworm or a curious reader seeking the next great page-turner, The Vintage Viking is your gateway to the best in science fiction and horror. Subscribe now to stay up to date with the latest reviews, book hauls, and discussions on the captivating worlds of science fiction and horror!

I mentioned Ira @sfwordsofwonder please go give him a follow and enjoy the content!


September Book reads are as follows :

1. Three body problem by Cixin Liu 

2. A deepness in the sky by Vernor Vinge

3. The pearl by John Steinbeck 

4. Pleasure Model by Christopher Rowley

5. Death Tour by David Michael 

6. Kaiju preservation society by John Scalzi

7. Bird Box by Josh Malerman

8. The Dark Tower 2: the drawing of the 3 by Stephen King

9. The Dark Tower 3: the wastelands by Stephen King

10. Firefly: Big Damn Heroes by James Lovegrove

Also mentioned books that will be read in the next month:

1. Let’s go play at the adams by Mendel Johnson

2. Wrath by Sharon Moalem and David Kraus

3. His pain by Wrath James White
